summary: Wire the persisted AI provider selection into the workspace rail so the setup-versus-configured state is truthful at runtime instead of always showing the unconfigured fallback.

decisions.decision: The AI rail derives configured state solely from the persisted provider discriminator; a provider name is not treated as a completed conversation capability.
decisions.rationale: This preserves AC-10.a's unconfigured zero-network state while preventing the product from showing setup guidance after the user has configured a provider.

testEvidence.criterion: The configured AI rail renders its ready state and the existing workspace layout suite remains green.
testEvidence.test: Run the focused WorkspaceFrame suite once after wiring and after correcting the exact message assertion.

red.outcome: RED: 1 of 11 tests failed because the new readiness assertion expected stale copy; the same run also exposed a 401-line UI source-policy violation after the first wiring shape.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 11 passed (11); Duration 1.04s.

testEvidence.criterion: The runtime wiring remains type-safe and source-policy clean.
testEvidence.test: Run TypeScript and repository lint/source policy once after the bounded correction.

red.outcome: RED: source policy reported src/ui/Workbench.tsx at 401 physical lines after the new prop was added.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false; pnpm.cmd lint
green.outcome: GREEN: TypeScript exited 0; Checked 422 files in 232ms with source policy passed.

questions: The configured rail still needs the message composer and provider invocation UI; this wiring unit only makes readiness state honest and does not claim AC-10.b/d complete.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
